module.name: Lone Worker
module.changelog: *1.0.11* The responder prompt now speaks the configured take-charge key dynamically (SayDigits) instead of having it baked into the audio, so the message stays correct if you change the "Key to take charge" setting. The call-post clip was re-recorded accordingly. *1.0.10* Feature codes are no longer hard-coded to 701/702/703: on a fresh install the module auto-assigns the first free codes (avoiding extensions and other modules feature codes), so it does not collide on PBXs where those codes are already in use. Existing/admin-set codes are preserved on upgrade. *1.0.9* Fix take-charge from a real responder phone: the alarmed operator extension is now passed to the confirm routine as an inherited channel variable (the U() Gosub argument resolved to the wrong value), so pressing the confirm key actually takes charge and the call log records who answered / who confirmed. *1.0.8* Session log now records the alarm call log: which responders were called (cascade), which responder answered, and which responder took charge — shown in each session's timeline and as a "taken charge by N" badge. *1.0.7* Each session now gets a stable, guaranteed-unique ID (assigned at arm, stored on the session and stamped on every one of its events); the Session log shows and searches by it. Sessions are grouped by that ID when present (robust), with reconstruction as a fallback for pre-1.0.7 history. *1.0.6* Add a per-session log (Session log view): browse active and past sessions, each expandable to its full timeline (arm, check-ins, reminders, alarm, re-calls, take-charge, disarm) with summary badges, duration, end reason; period filter (today/7d/30d/all) and live extension/name search. *1.0.5* Consistent navigation bar (Dashboard / Active sessions / Event history / Settings) on every view, so you can always return to the Dashboard; fixed the stale "Active sessions" link that pointed to the Dashboard. *1.0.4* Add a live Dashboard (auto-updating, no page refresh): KPI cards, live sessions with countdowns, announcement queue, system health, live event feed, plus a Reports section (today/7d/30d) with charts, per-operator stats, average take-charge time and CSV export. *1.0.3* Move the announcement queue to a DB table: the kvstore was cached per-process, so the CLI and the drain AGI clobbered each other's queue (lost/duplicated announcements). Single drain channel now enforced via live channel state. *1.0.2* Fix back-to-back announcements: a single paging channel now drains the whole queue (no re-paging collision), so two simultaneous alarms are both announced on the speakers. *1.0.1* Ship the Italian announcement audio built into the module (fixed set, not editable); add per-message "Play on speakers" preview buttons. *1.0.0* First release: native multi-operator lone worker monitoring.
module.description: Multi-operator "lone worker" / man-down safety module. Each operator arms a session from their own extension via a feature code (auto-assigned to a free code at install, default 701), gets reminders on the speakers, confirms presence (default 702) or disarms (default 703). If the timeout elapses with no confirmation, an alarm is announced on the speakers and calls are placed simultaneously to the configured responders (internal extensions and external numbers); the first to press the configured key takes charge and the other calls stop. Independent sessions per extension, serialised announcements, full event history.
